speaker: FENTON
text: I see I cannot get thy father’s love;

speaker: FENTON
text: Therefore no more turn me to him, sweet Nan.

speaker: ANNE
text: Alas, how then?

speaker: FENTON
text: Why, thou must be thyself.

speaker: FENTON
text: He doth object I am too great of birth,

speaker: FENTON
text: And that, my state being galled with my expense,

speaker: FENTON
text: I seek to heal it only by his wealth.

speaker: FENTON
text: Besides these, other bars he lays before me—

speaker: FENTON
text: My riots past, my wild societies—

speaker: FENTON
text: And tells me ’tis a thing impossible

speaker: FENTON
text: I should love thee but as a property.

speaker: ANNE
text: Maybe he tells you true.

speaker: FENTON
text: No, heaven so speed me in my time to come!

speaker: FENTON
text: Albeit I will confess thy father’s wealth

speaker: FENTON
text: Was the first motive that I wooed thee, Anne,

speaker: FENTON
text: Yet, wooing thee, I found thee of more value

speaker: FENTON
text: Than stamps in gold or sums in sealèd bags.

speaker: FENTON
text: And ’tis the very riches of thyself

speaker: FENTON
text: That now I aim at.

speaker: ANNE
text: Gentle Master Fenton,

speaker: ANNE
text: Yet seek my father’s love, still seek it, sir.

speaker: ANNE
text: If opportunity and humblest suit

speaker: ANNE
text: Cannot attain it, why then—hark you hither.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Break their talk, Mistress Quickly. My kinsman

speaker: SHALLOW
text: shall speak for himself.

speaker: SLENDER
text: I’ll make a shaft or a bolt on ’t. ’Slid, ’tis but

speaker: SLENDER
text: venturing.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Be not dismayed.

speaker: SLENDER
text: No, she shall not dismay me. I care not for

speaker: SLENDER
text: that, but that I am afeard.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Hark ye, Master Slender

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: would speak a word with you.

speaker: ANNE
text:
I come to him.
                 
                (
                Aside
                .
                )
               This is my father’s choice.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, what a world of vile ill-favored faults

speaker: ANNE
text: Looks handsome in three hundred pounds a year!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: And how does good Master Fenton?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Pray you, a word with you.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: She’s coming. To her, coz! O

speaker: SHALLOW
text: boy, thou hadst a father!

speaker: SLENDER
text: I had a father, Mistress Anne; my uncle can

speaker: SLENDER
text: tell you good jests of him.—Pray you, uncle, tell

speaker: SLENDER
text: Mistress Anne the jest how my father stole two

speaker: SLENDER
text: geese out of a pen, good uncle.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Mistress Anne, my cousin loves you.

speaker: SLENDER
text: Ay, that I do, as well as I love any woman in

speaker: SLENDER
text: Gloucestershire.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: He will maintain you like a gentlewoman.

speaker: SLENDER
text: Ay, that I will, come cut and longtail, under

speaker: SLENDER
text: the degree of a squire.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: He will make you a hundred and fifty

speaker: SHALLOW
text: pounds jointure.

speaker: ANNE
text: Good Master Shallow, let him woo for himself.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Marry, I thank you for it. I thank you for that

speaker: SHALLOW
text: good comfort.—She calls you, coz. I’ll leave you.

speaker: ANNE
text: Now, Master Slender.

speaker: SLENDER
text: Now, good Mistress Anne.

speaker: ANNE
text: What is your will?

speaker: SLENDER
text: My will? ’Od’s heartlings, that’s a pretty jest

speaker: SLENDER
text: indeed! I ne’er made my will yet, I thank heaven. I

speaker: SLENDER
text: am not such a sickly creature, I give heaven praise.

speaker: ANNE
text: I mean, Master Slender, what would you with

speaker: ANNE
text: me?

speaker: SLENDER
text: Truly, for mine own part, I would little or

speaker: SLENDER
text: nothing with you. Your father and my uncle hath

speaker: SLENDER
text: made motions. If it be my luck, so; if not, happy

speaker: SLENDER
text: man be his dole. They can tell you how things go

speaker: SLENDER
text: better than I can. You may ask your father.

speaker: SLENDER
text: Here he comes.

speaker: PAGE
text: Now, Master Slender.—Love him, daughter Anne.—

speaker: PAGE
text: Why, how now? What does Master Fenton here?

speaker: PAGE
text: You wrong me, sir, thus still to haunt my house.

speaker: PAGE
text: I told you, sir, my daughter is disposed of.

speaker: FENTON
text: Nay, Master Page, be not impatient.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Good Master Fenton, come not to my child.

speaker: PAGE
text: She is no match for you.

speaker: FENTON
text: Sir, will you hear me?

speaker: PAGE
text: No, good Master Fenton.—

speaker: PAGE
text: Come Master Shallow.—Come, son Slender, in.—

speaker: PAGE
text: Knowing my mind, you wrong me, Master Fenton.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Speak to Mistress Page.

speaker: FENTON
text: Good Mistress Page, for that I love your daughter

speaker: FENTON
text: In such a righteous fashion as I do,

speaker: FENTON
text: Perforce, against all checks, rebukes, and manners,

speaker: FENTON
text: I must advance the colors of my love

speaker: FENTON
text: And not retire. Let me have your good will.

speaker: ANNE
text: Good mother, do not marry me to yond fool.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: I mean it not; I seek you a better husband.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: That’s my master, Master Doctor.

speaker: ANNE
text: Alas, I had rather be set quick i’ th’ earth

speaker: ANNE
text: And bowled to death with turnips!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Come, trouble not yourself.—Good Master Fenton,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: I will not be your friend nor enemy.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: My daughter will I question how she loves you,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: And as I find her, so am I affected.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Till then, farewell, sir. She must needs go in;

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Her father will be angry.

speaker: FENTON
text: Farewell, gentle mistress.—Farewell, Nan.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: This is my doing now. “Nay,” said I,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: “will you cast away your child on a fool and a

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: physician? Look on Master Fenton.” This is my

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: doing.

speaker: FENTON
text: I thank thee; and I pray thee, once tonight

speaker: FENTON
text: Give my sweet Nan this ring. There’s for thy pains.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Now heaven send thee good fortune.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: A kind heart he hath. A woman would run through

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: fire and water for such a kind heart. But yet I

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: would my master had Mistress Anne, or I would

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Master Slender had her, or, in sooth, I would Master

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Fenton had her. I will do what I can for them all

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: three; for so I have promised and I’ll be as good as

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: my word—but speciously for Master Fenton. Well,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: I must of another errand to Sir John Falstaff from

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: my two mistresses. What a beast am I to slack it!
